1	from odoo import models, fields, api
2	
3	
4	class HrApplicant(models.Model):
5	    """Extend hr.applicant: show 'Schedule Interview' when stage name contains 'interview';
6	    open invite form with candidate/position defaults; and expose job kanban notes."""
7	
8	    _inherit = 'hr.applicant'
9	
10	    show_interview_button = fields.Boolean(
11	        compute='_compute_show_interview_button',
12	        store=False
13	    )
14	    job_kanban_notes = fields.Html(
15	        related='job_id.kanban_notes',
16	        string="Job Position Notes",
17	        readonly=False,
18	        store=False
19	    )
20	
21	    interview_invite_ids = fields.One2many(
22	        'recruitment.interview.invite',
23	        'applicant_id',
24	        string="Interview Invites"
25	    )
26	
27	    next_interview_date = fields.Datetime(
28	        string="Next Interview",
29	        compute="_compute_next_interview_date",
30	        store=True,
31	        help="Date of the next scheduled interview"
32	    )
33	
34	    @api.depends('interview_invite_ids.start_datetime', 'interview_invite_ids.status')
35	    def _compute_next_interview_date(self):
36	        for applicant in self:
37	            upcoming_interviews = self.env['recruitment.interview.invite'].search([
38	                ('applicant_id', '=', applicant.id),
39	                ('start_datetime', '>=', fields.Datetime.now()),
40	                ('status', 'not in', ['cancelled', 'rejected'])
41	            ], order='start_datetime asc', limit=1)
42	
43	            applicant.next_interview_date = upcoming_interviews.start_datetime if upcoming_interviews else False
44	
45	    @api.depends('stage_id', 'stage_id.name')
46	    def _compute_show_interview_button(self):
47	        for record in self:
48	            if record.stage_id and record.stage_id.name:
49	                record.show_interview_button = 'interview' in record.stage_id.name.lower()
50	            else:
51	                record.show_interview_button = False
52	
53	    def action_show_job_notes(self):
54	        """Open job position notes in a dialog (same simple form as Job kanban)."""
55	        self.ensure_one()
56	        return {
57	            'name': 'Job Notes',
58	            'type': 'ir.actions.act_window',
59	            'res_model': 'hr.job',
60	            'res_id': self.job_id.id,
61	            'view_mode': 'form',
62	            'view_id': self.env.ref('recruitment_interview_manager.hr_job_view_form_simple_notes').id,
63	            'target': 'new',
64	        }
65	
66	    def action_open_interview_invite(self):
67	        self.ensure_one()
68	
69	        partner = self.partner_id
70	        if not partner and self.email_from:
71	            partner = self.env['res.partner'].search([('email', '=', self.email_from)], limit=1)
72	
73	        if not partner:
74	            partner_vals = {
75	                'name': self.partner_name or self.name,
76	                'email': self.email_from,
77	                'phone': self.partner_phone,
78	                'mobile': self.partner_mobile,
79	                'is_company': False,
80	            }
81	            partner = self.env['res.partner'].create(partner_vals)
82	            self.write({'partner_id': partner.id})
83	
84	        default_context = {
85	            'default_candidate_id': partner.id,
86	            'default_applicant_id': self.id,
87	            'default_position_text': self.job_id.name if self.job_id else '',
88	        }
89	
90	        return {
91	            'name': 'Schedule Interview',
92	            'type': 'ir.actions.act_window',
93	            'res_model': 'recruitment.interview.invite',
94	            'view_mode': 'form',
95	            'target': 'new',
96	            'context': default_context,
97	        }

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<odoo>
    <record id="hr_applicant_view_form_inherit_interview_button" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">hr.applicant.form.inherit.interview.button</field>
        <field name="model">hr.applicant</field>
        <field name="inherit_id" ref="hr_recruitment.hr_applicant_view_form"/>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">
            <xpath expr="//header" position="inside">
                <field name="show_interview_button" invisible="1"/>
                <button name="action_open_interview_invite"
                        string="Schedule Interview"
                        type="object"
                        class="oe_highlight"
                        invisible="not show_interview_button"/>
            </xpath>

            <!-- Hide meeting button from button_box/stat buttons -->
            <xpath expr="//button[@name='action_create_meeting']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="invisible">1</attribute>
            </xpath>
        </field>
    </record>
</odoo>

Creates/syncs calendar events and emails 18 via Microsoft Graph (user or group calendar). Supports two-way sync: changes in 19 Teams/Outlook (time, attendees, cancellation) are pulled into Odoo by the cron. 20 21 Calendar operations use ``GraphClient.mail_acg`` (``recruitment.graph_organizer_email``). 22 The candidate HTML email uses ``GraphClient.mail_sender`` (optional shared mailbox via 23 ``recruitment.graph_mail_sender_email``); see README “Candidate email vs calendar”. 24 """ 25 _name = 'recruitment.interview.invite' 26 _description = 'Interview Invite' 27 _inherit = ['mail.thread', 'mail.activity.mixin'] 28 _rec_name = 'event_title' 29 _order = 'start_datetime desc, id desc' 30 31 # --- API CONFIGURATION --- 32 # Events are always created on the licensed user's calendar (so Teams links work). 33 # When recruitment.calendar_id is set, the group email is added as an attendee so the event also appears on the group calendar. 34 CALENDAR_EVENT_ENDPOINT = "/users/{user_id}/events" 35 ONLINE_MEETING_ENDPOINT = "/users/{user_id}/onlineMeetings" 36 SEND_MAIL_ENDPOINT = "/users/{user_id}/sendMail" 37 # --- CONSTANTS --- 38 MEETING_LOCATION_IN_PERSON = "2017 Texas Ave. El Paso, TX 79901" 39 40 # Attachment Paths 41 FILE_SOW_RFI = 'data/1. Creates/syncs calendar events and emails via Microsoft Graph (user or group calendar). Supports two-way sync: changes in Teams/Outlook (time, attendees, cancellation) are pulled into Odoo by the cron. Calendar operations use ``GraphClient.mail_acg`` (``recruitment.graph_organizer_email``). The candidate HTML email uses ``GraphClient.mail_sender`` (optional shared mailbox via ``recruitment.graph_mail_sender_email``); see README “Candidate email vs calendar”. """ _name = 'recruitment.interview.invite' _description = 'Interview Invite' _inherit = ['mail.thread', 'mail.activity.mixin'] _rec_name = 'event_title' _order = 'start_datetime desc, id desc' # --- API CONFIGURATION --- # Events are always created on the licensed user's calendar (so Teams links work). # When recruitment.calendar_id is set, the group email is added as an attendee so the event also appears on the group calendar. CALENDAR_EVENT_ENDPOINT = "/users/{user_id}/events" ONLINE_MEETING_ENDPOINT = "/users/{user_id}/onlineMeetings" SEND_MAIL_ENDPOINT = "/users/{user_id}/sendMail" # --- CONSTANTS --- MEETING_LOCATION_IN_PERSON = "2017 Texas Ave. El Paso, TX 79901" # Attachment Paths FILE_SOW_RFI = 'data/1. Hands on Assessment - SOW-RFI Construction.docx' FILE_EMPLOYMENT_APP = 'data/1. HRFORM-02 Employment Application.pdf' FILE_EXCEL_TEST = 'data/2.1 EXCEL TEST REV 2.19.25.xlsx' FILE_LOGO_SIGNATURE = 'data/logo.jpg' TYPING_TEST_TEXT = "    Please send screenshot of completed Typing Test. Typing Test link: https://www.typingtest.com/
" EXCEL_TEST_TEXT = "    Please complete the Attached Excel Test.
" SOW_RFI_TEST_TEXT = "    Please complete the Attached Hands On Assessment.
" event_title = fields.Char( string='Event Title', compute='_compute_event_title', store=True, readonly=False ) candidate_id = fields.Many2one('res.partner', string='Candidate', required=True, domain=[('is_company', '=', False)]) candidate_email = fields.Char(string='Email Address', related='candidate_id.email', readonly=True, store=True) applicant_id = fields.Many2one('hr.applicant', string='Applicant', help='Link to the recruitment applicant record') job_id = fields.Many2one('hr.job', string='Job Position', related='applicant_id.job_id', store=True, readonly=False) position_text = fields.Char(string='Position Applied For') start_datetime = fields.Datetime(string='Start', required=True) duration = fields.Selection([ ('0.5', '30 Minutes'), ('1.0', '1 Hour'), ('1.5', '1.5 Hours'), ('2.0', '2 Hours'), ], string='Duration', default='1.0', required=True) end_datetime = fields.Datetime(string='End', compute='_compute_end_datetime', store=True, readonly=True) meeting_type = fields.Selection([ ('Teams', 'Microsoft Teams'), ('In Person', 'In Person'), ], string='Meeting Type', default='Teams', required=True) # Test Checkboxes include_typing_test = fields.Boolean(string='Include Typing Test', default=False) include_excel_test = fields.Boolean(string='Include Excel Test', default=False) include_sow_rfi_test = fields.Boolean(string='Include SOW RFI Test', default=False) include_employment_application = fields.Boolean(string='Include Employment Application', default=False) # Attendees send_to_recruitment = fields.Boolean(string='Send to Recruitment', default=True, help='Send invitation to recruiting@acglp.com') acg_attendees_ids = fields.Many2many('hr.employee', string='ACG Attendees') # Candidate CV Fields candidate_cv = fields.Binary(string='Candidate CV', attachment=True) candidate_cv_filename = fields.Char(string='CV Filename') # Status status = fields.Selection([ ('draft', 'Draft'), ('sent', 'Sent'), ('confirmed', 'Confirmed'), ('rejected', 'Rejected'), ('done', 'Done'), ('cancelled', 'Cancelled'), ], string='Status', default='draft')

                <attribute name="invisible">1</attribute>

            <!-- Add Interviews tab to notebook -->
            <xpath expr="//notebook" position="inside">
                <page string="Interviews" name="interviews">
                    <field name="interview_invite_ids" nolabel="1">
                        <list string="Interview Meetings">
                            <field name="event_title"/>
                            <field name="start_datetime"/>
                            <field name="end_datetime"/>
                            <field name="meeting_type"/>
                            <field name="status" widget="badge"
                                   decoration-success="status == 'confirmed'"
                                   decoration-info="status == 'sent'"
                                   decoration-warning="status == 'draft'"
                                   decoration-danger="status == 'cancelled'"/>
                        </list>
                    </field>
                </page>
            </xpath>
        </field>
    </record>

                        invisible="not show_interview_button"/>

content.text:
Done! I've added a new "Interviews" tab to the applicant form view. The tab displays all scheduled interview meetings in a list with the following information:

- **Event Title**
- **Start DateTime**
- **End DateTime**  
- **Meeting Type** (Teams/In Person)
- **Status** (with color-coded badges: green for confirmed, blue for sent, orange for draft, red for cancelled)

Now when you open an applicant record, you'll see an "Interviews" tab where you can view all scheduled interview meetings at a glance without leaving the applicant form.

    'description': """
HR Recruitment Enhanced
=======================

This module extends the standard Odoo recruitment module with additional features:

* Duplicate job position detection during creation
* Simplified job creation form
* Quick access to job configuration settings from kanban view
* Publication history tracking for job positions
* Phone screen question management
* Quiz question management for candidate assessment
* Recruitment status field for filtering open/closed positions
* Recruitment checklist templates for standardized hiring workflows

Features:
---------
- Automatic duplicate detection when creating job positions
- Clean, streamlined job creation interface
- Comprehensive audit trail for job publication status
- Structured phone screening questions with expected answers
- Quiz builder for candidate assessments
- Enhanced filtering and status visibility
- Recruitment checklist templates with SharePoint integration
- Automatic checklist population for each applicant
- Track completion status, responsible user, and result locations
- Template approval workflow (Draft → Pending Approval → Approved)
- Attachment support for templates when SharePoint is unavailable
- Download template attachments directly from candidate checklist
- Upload result attachments for each checklist item
- Vacancy wizard when opening positions to set recruitment targets
- Manager request date tracking and KPI metrics (days open, days to close)

- Next interview date display in applicant list/kanban views
- Recruitment KPI dashboard with analytics and PDF reports
- Disabled PDF preview in attachments (prevents chatter from being pushed down)
- All applicant attachments visible to all recruitment users

uploads)'), 120 ('both', 'Both (HR staff and candidate)'), 121 ], 122 string='Uploaded By', 123 default='internal', 124 required=True, 125 help='Who is responsible for uploading the completed document for this item', 126 ) 127 128 notes = fields.Text( 129 string='Notes', 130 help='Additional notes or comments about this checklist item' 131 ) 132 133 rating = fields.Selection( 134 [ 135 ('0', 'Not Rated (0)'), 136 ('5', 'Below Expectations (5)'), 137 ('10', 'Meets Expectations (10)'), 138 ], 139 string='Evaluation Rating', 140 help='Evaluation score for this checklist item (0, 5, or 10 points)', 141 tracking=True, 142 ) 143 144 rating_notes = fields.Text( 145 string='Rating Notes', 146 help='Comments or feedback about the rating', 147 tracking=True, 148 ) 149 150 auto_completed_by_candidate = fields.Boolean( 151 string='Auto-Completed by Candidate Upload', 152 default=False, 153 readonly=True, 154 help='Indicates this item was automatically marked complete action_view_template_attachments(self): 162 self.ensure_one() 163 return { 164 'name': f'Template Attachments: {self.name}', 165 'type': 'ir.actions.act_window', 166 'res_model': 'ir.attachment', 167 'view_mode': 'list,form', 168 'domain': [('id', 'in', self.template_attachment_ids.ids)], 169 'context': {'create': False} 170 } 171 172 @api.model_create_multi 173 def create(self, vals_list): 174 for vals in vals_list: 175 if vals.get('completed') and not vals.get('completed_by'): 176 vals['completed_by'] = self.env.user.id 177 vals['completed_date'] = fields.Datetime.now() 178 return super().create(vals_list) 179 180 def write(self, vals): 181 if vals.get('completed'): 182 if not self.completed: 183 vals['completed_by'] = self.env.user.id 184 vals['completed_date'] = fields.Datetime.now() 185 elif 'completed' in vals and not vals['completed']: 186 vals['completed_by'] = False 187 vals['completed_date'] = False 188 189 return super().write(vals) 190 191 def action_mark_complete_by_candidate(self): 192 """Mark checklist item as completed when candidate uploads document. 193 194 This method is called from the portal controller when a candidate uploads 195 a document. It auto-completes the item and notifies HR users. 196 """ 197 self.ensure_one() 198 199 if not self.completed: 200 self.sudo().write({ 201 'completed': True, 202 'completed_by': self.env.user.id, 203 'completed_date': fields.Datetime.now(), 204 'auto_completed_by_candidate': True, 205 }) 206 207 # Notify recruitment users about the upload 208 self._notify_hr_users_of_candidate_upload() 209 210 def _notify_hr_users_of_candidate_upload(self): 211 """Send notification to HR users when candidate uploads a document.""" 212 self.ensure_one() 213 214 # Get recruitment users (users with recruitment access) 215 recruitment_group = self.env.ref('hr_recruitment.group_hr_recruitment_user', raise_if_not_found=False) 216 if not recruitment_group: 217 return 218 219 # Build notification message 220 candidate_name = self.applicant_id.partner_name or 'Candidate' 221 job_title = self.applicant_id.job_id.name if self.applicant_id.job_id else 'Position' 222 223 notification_body = _( 224 '

Candidate Document Uploaded

' 225 '
    ' 226 '
  • Candidate: %(candidate)s
    • ' 227 '
  • Position: %(position)s
    • ' 228 '
  • Checklist Item: %(item)s
    • ' 229 '
  • Status: Auto-completed ✓
    • ' 230 '
' 231 '

' 232 'View Application

' 233 ) % { 234 'candidate': candidate_name, 235 'position': job_title, 236 'item': self.name, 237 'applicant_id': self.applicant_id.id, 238 } 239 240 # Post message to applicant with notification to recruitment users 241 self.applicant_id.message_post( 242 body=notification_body, 243 subject=_('Candidate uploaded: %s') % self.name, 244 message_type='notification', 245 subtype_xmlid='mail.mt_note', 246 partner_ids=recruitment_group.users.mapped('partner_id').ids, 247 ) 248

from odoo import models, fields, api class HrApplicantChecklistItem(models.Model): _name = 'hr.applicant.checklist.item' _description = 'Applicant Checklist Item' _inherit = ['mail.thread'] _order = 'sequence, id' applicant_id = fields.Many2one( 'hr.applicant', string='Applicant', required=True, ondelete='cascade' ) template_id = fields.Many2one( 'hr.job.checklist.template', string='Template', ondelete='set null', help='Reference to the template this item was created from' ) name = fields.Char( string='Checklist Item', required=True, help='Name of the checklist item' ) sequence = fields.Integer( string='Sequence', default=10, help='Order of this checklist item' ) completed = fields.Boolean( string='Completed', default=False ) completed_by = fields.Many2one( 'res.users', string='Completed By', readonly=True, help='User who marked this item as completed' ) completed_date = fields.Datetime( string='Completed Date', readonly=True, help='Date and time when this item was completed' ) result_url = fields.Char( string='Result URL', help='URL to the result document stored in SharePoint or other location' ) result_attachment = fields.Binary( string='Result Document', attachment=True, help='Upload the completed document' ) result_attachment_filename = fields.Char( string='Result Filename' ) result_attachment_ids = fields.Many2many( 'ir.attachment', 'hr_applicant_checklist_item_attachment_rel', 'checklist_item_id', 'attachment_id', string='Multiple Result Attachments', help='Upload multiple completed documents for this checklist item' ) result_attachment_count = fields.Integer( string='Result Attachment Count', compute='_compute_result_attachment_count' ) sharepoint_url = fields.Char( string='Template URL', help='URL to the template document (from template)', related='template_id.sharepoint_url', readonly=True ) template_attachment = fields.Binary( string='Template Document', related='template_id.attachment', readonly=True, help='Template document from job position' ) template_attachment_filename = fields.Char( string='Template Filename', related='template_id.attachment_filename', readonly=True ) template_attachment_ids = fields.Many2many( string='Template Attachments', related='template_id.attachment_ids', readonly=True, help='Attachments from the template' ) template_attachment_count = fields.Integer( string='Template attachment count', related='template_id.attachment_count', readonly=True ) upload_by = fields.Selection( [ ('internal', 'Internal (HR staff only)'), ('candidate', 'Candidate (candidate uploads)'), ('both', 'Both (HR staff and candidate)'), ], string='Uploaded By', default='internal', required=True, help='Who is responsible for uploading the completed document for this item', ) notes = fields.Text( string='Notes', help='Additional notes or comments about this checklist item' ) rating = fields.Selection( [ ('0', 'Not Rated (0)'), ('5', 'Below Expectations (5)'), ('10', 'Meets Expectations (10)'), ], string='Evaluation Rating', help='Evaluation score for this checklist item (0, 5, or 10 points)', tracking=True, ) rating_notes = fields.Text( string='Rating Notes', help='Comments or feedback about the rating', tracking=True, ) auto_completed_by_candidate = fields.Boolean( string='Auto-Completed by Candidate Upload', default=False, readonly=True, help='Indicates this item was automatically marked complete when candidate uploaded document', ) def _compute_result_attachment_count(self): for record in self: record.result_attachment_count = len(record.result_attachment_ids) def action_view_template_attachments(self): self.ensure_one() return { 'name': f'Template Attachments: {self.name}', 'type': 'ir.actions.act_window', 'res_model': 'ir.attachment', 'view_mode': 'list,form', 'domain': [('id', 'in', self.template_attachment_ids.ids)], 'context': {'create': False} } @api.model_create_multi def create(self, vals_list): for vals in vals_list: if vals.get('completed') and not vals.get('completed_by'): vals['completed_by'] = self.env.user.id vals['completed_date'] = fields.Datetime.now() return super().create(vals_list) def write(self, vals): if vals.get('completed'): if not self.completed: vals['completed_by'] = self.env.user.id vals['completed_date'] = fields.Datetime.now() elif 'completed' in vals and not vals['completed']: vals['completed_by'] = False vals['completed_date'] = False return super().write(vals) def action_mark_complete_by_candidate(self): """Mark checklist item as completed when candidate uploads document. This method is called from the portal controller when a candidate uploads a document. It auto-completes the item and notifies HR users. """ self.ensure_one() if not self.completed: self.sudo().write({ 'completed': True, 'completed_by': self.env.user.id, 'completed_date': fields.Datetime.now(), 'auto_completed_by_candidate': True, }) # Notify recruitment users about the upload self._notify_hr_users_of_candidate_upload() def _notify_hr_users_of_candidate_upload(self): """Send notification to HR users when candidate uploads a document.""" self.ensure_one() # Get recruitment users (users with recruitment access) recruitment_group = self.env.ref('hr_recruitment.group_hr_recruitment_user', raise_if_not_found=False) if not recruitment_group: return # Build notification message candidate_name = self.applicant_id.partner_name or 'Candidate' job_title = self.applicant_id.job_id.name if self.applicant_id.job_id else 'Position' notification_body = _( '

Candidate Document Uploaded

' '
    ' '
  • Candidate: %(candidate)s
    • ' '
  • Position: %(position)s
    • ' '
  • Checklist Item: %(item)s
    • ' '
  • Status: Auto-completed ✓
    • ' '
' '

' 'View Application

' ) % { 'candidate': candidate_name, 'position': job_title, 'item': self.name, 'applicant_id': self.applicant_id.id, } # Post message to applicant with notification to recruitment users self.applicant_id.message_post( body=notification_body, subject=_('Candidate uploaded: %s') % self.name, message_type='notification', subtype_xmlid='mail.mt_note', partner_ids=recruitment_group.users.mapped('partner_id').ids, )

from odoo import models, fields


class HrJobChecklistTemplate(models.Model):
    _name = 'hr.job.checklist.template'
    _description = 'Job Position Checklist Template'
    _order = 'sequence, id'
    _inherit = ['mail.thread', 'mail.activity.mixin']

    job_id = fields.Many2one(
        'hr.job',
        string='Job Position',
        required=False,
        ondelete='cascade',
        help='Leave empty to create a global default template reusable by any job position',
    )

    name = fields.Char(
        string='Checklist Item',
        required=True,
        tracking=True,
        help='Name of the checklist item (e.g., Job Posting Template, Resume Filter Checklist)'
    )

    sequence = fields.Integer(
        string='Sequence',
        default=10,
        help='Order of this checklist item'
    )

    status = fields.Selection(
        [
            ('draft', 'Draft'),
            ('pending_approval', 'Pending Approval'),
            ('approved', 'Approved')
        ],
        string='Status',
        default='draft',
        required=True,
        tracking=True,
        help='Status of this checklist template'
    )

    sharepoint_url = fields.Char(
        string='SharePoint URL',
        tracking=True,
        help='URL to the document template stored in SharePoint'
    )

    attachment = fields.Binary(
        string='Attached Document',
        attachment=True,
        help='Upload a single document file'
    )

    attachment_filename = fields.Char(
        string='Filename'
    )

    attachment_ids = fields.Many2many(
        'ir.attachment',
        'hr_job_checklist_template_attachment_rel',
        'checklist_template_id',
        'attachment_id',
        string='Multiple Attachments',
        help='Upload multiple template documents if needed'
    )

    attachment_count = fields.Integer(
        string='Template attachments count',
        compute='_compute_attachment_count'
    )

    upload_by = fields.Selection(
        [
            ('internal', 'Internal (HR staff only)'),
            ('candidate', 'Candidate (candidate uploads)'),
            ('both', 'Both (HR staff and candidate)'),
        ],
        string='Uploaded By',
        default='internal',
        required=True,
        help='Who is responsible for uploading the completed document for this item',
    )

    description = fields.Text(
        string='Description',
        help='Additional information about this checklist item'
    )

    def _compute_attachment_count(self):
        for record in self:
            record.attachment_count = len(record.attachment_ids)

from odoo import models, fields


class HrJobChecklistTemplate(models.Model):
    _name = 'hr.job.checklist.template'
    _description = 'Job Position Checklist Template'
    _order = 'sequence, id'
    _inherit = ['mail.thread', 'mail.activity.mixin']

    job_id = fields.Many2one(
        'hr.job',
        string='Job Position',
        required=False,
        ondelete='cascade',
        help='Leave empty to create a global default template reusable by any job position',
    )

    name = fields.Char(
        string='Checklist Item',
        required=True,
        tracking=True,
        help='Name of the checklist item (e.g., Job Posting Template, Resume Filter Checklist)'
    )

    sequence = fields.Integer(
        string='Sequence',
        default=10,
        help='Order of this checklist item'
    )

    status = fields.Selection(
        [
            ('draft', 'Draft'),
            ('pending_approval', 'Pending Approval'),
            ('approved', 'Approved')
        ],
        string='Status',
        default='draft',
        required=True,
        tracking=True,
        help='Status of this checklist template'
    )

    sharepoint_url = fields.Char(
        string='SharePoint URL',
        tracking=True,
        help='URL to the document template stored in SharePoint'
    )

    attachment = fields.Binary(
        string='Attached Document',
        attachment=True,
        help='Upload a single document file'
    )

    attachment_filename = fields.Char(
        string='Filename'
    )

    attachment_ids = fields.Many2many(
        'ir.attachment',
        'hr_job_checklist_template_attachment_rel',
        'checklist_template_id',
        'attachment_id',
        string='Multiple Attachments',
        help='Upload multiple template documents if needed'
    )

    attachment_count = fields.Integer(
        string='Template attachments count',
        compute='_compute_attachment_count'
    )

    upload_by = fields.Selection(
        [
            ('internal', 'Internal (HR staff only)'),
            ('candidate', 'Candidate (candidate uploads)'),
            ('both', 'Both (HR staff and candidate)'),
        ],
        string='Uploaded By',
        default='internal',
        required=True,
        help='Who is responsible for uploading the completed document for this item',
    )

    description = fields.Text(
        string='Description',
        help='Additional information about this checklist item'
    )

    def _compute_attachment_count(self):
        for record in self:
            record.attachment_count = len(record.attachment_ids)

global_templates = self.env['hr.job.checklist.template'].search([('job_id', '=', False)])

        if global_templates:
            for tmpl in global_templates:
                self.env['hr.job.checklist.template'].create({
                    'job_id': self.id,
                    'name': tmpl.name,
                    'sequence': tmpl.sequence,
                    'upload_by': tmpl.upload_by,
                    'status': tmpl.status,
                    'sharepoint_url': tmpl.sharepoint_url,
                    'description': tmpl.description,
                })
            count = len(global_templates)
            message = f'{count} checklist template(s) copied from global defaults.'
        else:
            hardcoded = [
                {'sequence': 1, 'name': 'Job Posting Template'},
                {'sequence': 2, 'name': 'Resume Filter Checklist'},

content.text:
Perfect! I've successfully added the "Owner" field to track who is responsible for each checklist item. Here's what was implemented:

## Changes Made:

### 1. **Checklist Item Model** (`hr_applicant_checklist_item.py`)
   - Added `owner_id` field (Many2one to `res.users`) with tracking enabled

### 2. **Checklist Template Model** (`hr_job_checklist_template.py`)
   - Added `owner_id` field so templates can have a default owner
   - When checklist items are created from templates, the owner is automatically copied

### 3. **Checklist Item Views** (`hr_applicant_checklist_item_views.xml`)
   - Added owner field to the list view (visible by default)
   - Added owner field to the form view
   - Added owner to search filters for easy filtering
   - Added "Group By Owner" option

### 4. **Applicant View** (`hr_applicant_views_inherit.xml`)
   - Added owner column to the checklist items list within the applicant form
   - Added owner field to the inline form view

### 5. **Template Views** (`hr_job_checklist_template_views.xml`)
   - Added owner field to template list and form views
   - Added owner to search and group by filters

### 6. **Logic Updates**
   - Modified `action_populate_checklist_items()` in `hr_applicant.py` to copy the owner from template to checklist item
   - Modified `action_populate_default_checklist()` in `hr_job.py` to include owner when copying global templates

Now you can:
- Assign an owner to each checklist item
- Set a default owner on templates that will be copied to new checklist items
- Filter and group checklist items by owner
- See who is responsible for each activity directly in the applicant form

content: Added two features: an "Interviews" tab in the applicant form to display scheduled interview meetings, and an "Owner" field to checklist items and templates to track who is responsible for each activity. Both are ready to use. (disable recaps in /config)

content.text:
Done! I've added the owner field to the job position's recruitment checklist views. The changes include:

1. **Checklist List in Job Form** - Added the `owner_id` column (set to optional="show" so it's visible by default)
2. **Inline Checklist Form in Job** - Added both `upload_by` and `owner_id` fields to the form view

Now when you view a job position and look at the "Recruitment Checklist" tab, you'll see the Owner column in the list and be able to edit it directly. The owner field will also appear in the detailed form when you open a checklist template item from the job position.

message.content: if is modify there will copy to the applicatan?

content.text:
Good question! Currently, the owner field is only copied **when the checklist items are first created** from the template. If you modify the owner in the template after items have already been created for applicants, those existing checklist items **will NOT** be automatically updated.

This means:
- ✅ **New applicants**: When you load the checklist for a new applicant, they will get the current owner from the template
- ❌ **Existing applicants**: Their checklist items keep the original owner value

Would you like me to add one of these options?

questions.question: How should changes to the template owner affect existing applicant checklist items?

options.label: Keep as-is (no automatic updates)
options.description: Template changes only affect new applicants. Existing checklist items remain unchanged. This gives you full control over each applicant's checklist.

options.label: Add an 'Update from Template' button
options.description: Add a button on the job position to update all existing applicant checklist items from the current template values (owner, upload_by, etc.). You decide when to sync.

options.label: Auto-update when template changes
options.description: Automatically update all linked checklist items whenever the template owner is changed. Changes propagate immediately to all applicants.

content.text:
Perfect! I've made the changes. Here's what's now in place:

## Workflow (Default → Job → Applicant)

1. **Default Checklist Templates** (the only menu you'll see now)
   - Set up your standard checklist items with owners here

2. **Job Position** → Click "Load Default Checklist Templates" button
   - Copies all fields including `owner_id` from default templates to job-specific templates

3. **Applicant** → Click "Load Recruitment Checklist" button  
   - Copies all fields including `owner_id` from job templates to applicant checklist items

## Menu Changes
- ✅ **Kept**: Default Checklist Templates menu (only visible menu)
- ❌ **Hidden**: Checklist Templates menu (job-specific)
- ❌ **Hidden**: Applicant Checklist Items menu (managed through applicant form)

The owner field now flows through the entire process:
**Default Templates → Job Templates → Applicant Items**

Each step copies the owner when you use the "Load" buttons, so you manage owners at the default template level and they propagate down through the workflow.

from odoo import models, fields, api


class HrJob(models.Model):
    _inherit = 'hr.job'

    recruitment_status = fields.Selection(
        [('open', 'Open'), ('closed', 'Closed')],
        string='Recruitment Status',
        default='open',
        tracking=True,
        help="Indicates whether this position is actively recruiting"
    )

    publication_history_ids = fields.One2many(
        'hr.job.publication.history',
        'job_id',
        string='Publication History'
    )

    phone_screen_question_ids = fields.One2many(
        'hr.job.phone.screen.question',
        'job_id',
        string='Phone Screen Questions'
    )

    quiz_question_ids = fields.One2many(
        'hr.job.quiz.question',
        'job_id',
        string='Quiz Questions'
    )

    checklist_template_ids = fields.One2many(
        'hr.job.checklist.template',
        'job_id',
        string='Recruitment Checklist Templates'
    )

    similar_job_ids = fields.Many2many(
        'hr.job',
        compute='_compute_similar_jobs',
        string='Similar Positions'
    )

    sharepoint_url = fields.Char(
        string="SharePoint URL",
        help="Link to SharePoint folder for this position"
    )

    manager_request_date = fields.Date(

Perfect! I've completed all the requested changes. Here's what was implemented:

## 1. **Portal Job Description Field**
   - Added `job_description_portal` field to `hr.job` model (HTML field)
   - Visible in the **Job Posting** tab with a dedicated section

## 2. **Job Description Tab in Applicant Form**
   - Added new **"Job Description"** tab in the applicant form
   - Displays the `job_description_portal` from the linked job position
   - Read-only for interviewers to easily reference during interviews
   - Only visible when a job position is linked to the applicant

## 3. **Hidden "0 New Applications" Button**
   - The button is now hidden from the job position kanban view
   - Cleaner interface without the extra button

## Changes Summary:

### Files Modified:
- `models/hr_job.py` - Added `job_description_portal` field
- `views/hr_job_views.xml` - Added field to Job Posting tab and hidden the button
- `views/hr_applicant_views_inherit.xml` - Added Job Description tab

### Workflow:
1. Edit job position → Go to **Job Posting** tab → Fill in "Job Description (Portal)"
2. This description is now visible to candidates in the portal
3. Interviewers can view it in the applicant form under the **Job Description** tab

content.text:
Perfect! I've restructured the portal template. Now the layout shows:

## Portal Application Page Structure:

### **Top Section** (Application Information)
1. Application header with job position title
2. Applicant details (name, email, phone)
3. Application date and current stage
4. Next interview date (if scheduled)

### **Middle Section** (Documents & Actions)
5. Required documents checklist with upload forms
6. Additional document upload section
7. Already uploaded documents table

### **Bottom Section** (Job Description)
8. **Job Description (Portal)** - Displayed at the very bottom with:
   - Clear heading with icon
   - Styled container with border and background
   - Full HTML content from `job_description_portal` field

## Key Changes:
- ✅ All form fields remain at the top
- ✅ Job description moved to the bottom
- ✅ Uses the new `job_description_portal` field instead of the regular `description`
- ✅ Only shows if the field has content
- ✅ Better visual separation with horizontal rule and spacing

Now candidates will see all their application information first, handle document uploads, and then read the detailed job description at the bottom of the page.

content.text:
Perfect! I've significantly improved the candidate email content. Here are the enhancements:

## Email Improvements:

### **1. Interview Requirements Section**
- ✅ Clear **"Interview Requirements"** heading in bold
- ✅ Professional introduction explaining the portal and credentials
- ✅ Structured as a bulleted list with proper HTML formatting
- ✅ Each requirement is clearly labeled:
  - **Typing Test** - with direct link
  - **Excel Test** - with instructions note
  - **Hands-On Assessment** - clear description
  - **Employment Application** - upload instructions

### **2. Better Formatting**
- ✅ HTML `<ul>` list with proper spacing
- ✅ Bold labels for each requirement type
- ✅ Clickable link for typing test
- ✅ Styled note section for upload issues

### **3. Enhanced Contact Section**
- ✅ Clear "Questions or Need Help?" heading
- ✅ Email and phone with emojis (📧 📞) for visual clarity
- ✅ More professional and welcoming tone
- ✅ Positioned before the portal credentials

### **4. Internal Staff Link**
- ✅ Moved to the very bottom with horizontal separator
- ✅ Lighter gray styling to indicate it's administrative
- ✅ Clear "For ACG internal use only" label

### **Before vs After:**

**Before:** Plain text instructions mixed together
**After:** Professional, well-structured sections with:
- Clear headings
- Bulleted requirements
- Better visual hierarchy
- Professional styling
- Helpful notes

The email now provides a much better candidate experience with clear, actionable instructions!

file.content:
from odoo import models, fields, api
import secrets


class HrApplicant(models.Model):
    _inherit = 'hr.applicant'

    phone_screen_answer_ids = fields.One2many(
        'hr.applicant.phone.screen.answer',
        'applicant_id',
        string='Phone Screen Answers'
    )

    quiz_answer_ids = fields.One2many(
        'hr.applicant.quiz.answer',
        'applicant_id',
        string='Quiz Answers'
    )

    checklist_item_ids = fields.One2many(
        'hr.applicant.checklist.item',
        'applicant_id',
        string='Recruitment Checklist'
    )

    quiz_token = fields.Char(
        string='Quiz Access Token',
        copy=False,
        readonly=True
    )

    quiz_completed = fields.Boolean(
        string='Quiz Completed',
        default=False
    )

    portal_user_id = fields.Many2one(
        'res.users',
        string='Portal User',
        help='Portal user account for this applicant',
        compute='_compute_portal_user_id',
        store=True
    )

    has_portal_access = fields.Boolean(
        string='Has Portal Access',
        compute='_compute_has_portal_access',
        store=True
    )

- ✅ `views/hr_applicant_views_inherit.xml` - Simplified the Job Description tab

**Try upgrading the module again** - it should now work without errors. The Job Description tab will display the portal job description from the linked job position.

aiTitle: Add interview meetings tab to applicant form

content.text:
Done! I've moved the Candidate Portal Access section to appear right after the interview requirements and before the "Questions or Need Help?" section.

## New Email Structure:

1. **Greeting** - Hi [Candidate Name]
2. **Interview Details** - Date, Time, Location
3. **Interview Requirements** - Tests and documents to complete (if any)
4. **📋 Candidate Portal Access** ← **Moved here** (login credentials)
5. **Questions or Need Help?** - Contact information
6. **Thank you message**
7. **Internal Staff Link** - (bottom, for ACG staff only)
8. **Signature**

This flow makes more sense because:
- ✅ Candidates learn what they need to do (requirements)
- ✅ Immediately get access credentials to complete those requirements
- ✅ Then see where to get help if needed

The email now has a more logical progression from requirements → credentials → support contact.

from odoo import models, fields, api


class RecruitmentInterviewPortalWizard(models.TransientModel):
    _name = 'recruitment.interview.portal.wizard'
    _description = 'Portal Credentials Wizard'

    interview_invite_id = fields.Many2one(
        'recruitment.interview.invite',
        string='Interview Invite',
        required=True
    )

    applicant_id = fields.Many2one(
        'hr.applicant',
        string='Applicant',
        required=True
    )

    applicant_name = fields.Char(
        related='applicant_id.partner_name',
        string='Candidate Name',
        readonly=True
    )

    applicant_email = fields.Char(
        related='applicant_id.email_from',
        string='Email',
        readonly=True
    )

    def action_create_and_send(self):
        """Create portal credentials and send the invitation."""
        self.ensure_one()

        # Create portal access for the applicant
        self.applicant_id.action_grant_portal_access()

        # Send the invitation
        return self.interview_invite_id._send_invite_internal()

    def action_skip_and_send(self):
        """Skip portal creation and send invitation anyway."""
        self.ensure_one()

        # Send the invitation without portal credentials
        return self.interview_invite_id._send_invite_internal()

from . import controllers
from . import models
from . import migration_utils


def pre_init_hook(cr):
    """Fix NULL ``candidate_id`` on ``recruitment_interview_invite`` before the registry applies NOT NULL.

    Mirrors ``migrations/*/pre-migrate.py`` via ``migration_utils.fix_null_recruitment_interview_candidate_ids``
    (Odoo 18 ``hr_candidate`` path, legacy ``hr_applicant.partner_id``, fallback partner, delete orphans).
    """
    migration_utils.fix_null_recruitment_interview_candidate_ids(cr)

lines: """Internal method to actually send the invitation after validation."""

content.text:
Perfect! I've added portal credentials validation before sending interview invitations. Here's what was implemented:

## New Validation Flow:

### **Before Sending Interview:**
1. System checks if the applicant has portal credentials
2. If **NO credentials found** → Shows validation wizard
3. If **credentials exist** → Sends invitation normally

### **Portal Credentials Wizard:**

**Warning Message:**
- 🚨 "Portal Credentials Not Found"
- Explains why portal access is needed:
  - View application status
  - Upload required documents
  - Complete assessments
  - Access interview details

**Shows Candidate Info:**
- Name
- Email

**Two Action Buttons:**

1. **"Create Portal Credentials & Send"** (Primary)
   - Creates portal access for the candidate
   - Automatically sends invitation with login credentials
   - ✅ Recommended option

2. **"Skip & Send Anyway"** (Secondary)
   - Sends invitation without portal credentials
   - ⚠️ Candidate won't be able to access the portal
   - Use only if portal access isn't needed

3. **"Cancel"** - Returns to interview form without sending

## Files Created/Modified:

### **New Files:**
- `wizard/portal_credentials_wizard.py` - Wizard model
- `wizard/portal_credentials_wizard_view.xml` - Wizard UI
- `wizard/__init__.py` - Wizard initialization

### **Modified Files:**
- `models/interview_invite.py` - Added validation and split send logic
- `__init__.py` - Added wizard import
- `__manifest__.py` - Added wizard view
- `security/ir.model.access.csv` - Added wizard permissions

The system now ensures recruiters are aware when candidates don't have portal access and provides an easy way to create it!

``` ## **What Changed:** 1. ✅ **Inherits** from `base.res_config_settings_view_form` 2. ✅ Adds Daily Reports as a **new section** in settings 3. ✅ Uses standard Odoo settings layout with `app_settings_block` 4. ✅ Removed `view_id` from action (uses standard settings view) 5. ✅ All other settings sections remain visible ## **Result:** Now when users click "Configuration": - ✅ See the **full Settings menu** with all sections - ✅ "Daily Reports" appears as a **new section** alongside others - ✅ Can access General Settings, Users, Companies, etc. - ✅ Follows standard Odoo settings UX patterns The settings menu is no longer replaced - Daily Reports configuration is properly integrated!

aiTitle: Add interview meetings tab to applicant form

content.text:
Fixed! I've updated it to use **Odoo 18's proper settings structure**. The key changes:

## What Changed:

### **1. Correct Odoo 18 Structure:**
```xml
<!-- Odoo 18 uses <block> and <setting> tags -->
<xpath expr="//form//sheet" position="inside">
    <block title="Daily Reports" groups="base.group_system">
        <setting id="daily_scheduling_user_setting"
                 string="Daily Report Scheduling User"
                 help="...">
            <field name="daily_scheduling_user_id"/>
        </setting>
    </block>
</xpath>
```

### **2. Key Differences from Previous Attempt:**
- ✅ Uses `//form//sheet` xpath (works in Odoo 18)
- ✅ Uses `<block>` tag for settings sections (Odoo 18)
- ✅ Uses `<setting>` tag for individual settings (Odoo 18)
- ✅ Removed deprecated `app_settings_block` class
- ✅ Simplified layout to match Odoo 18 patterns

### **Result:**
Now the Daily Reports configuration will:
- ✅ Appear as a proper settings block in the Settings view
- ✅ Not replace the entire settings menu
- ✅ Follow Odoo 18's settings UI conventions
- ✅ Show alongside all other settings sections

**Try upgrading the module now** - it should work without the xpath error!

aiTitle: Add interview meetings tab to applicant form

content.text:
I've changed the approach to avoid xpath issues completely. Now it's a **standalone settings form** that doesn't try to inherit: ## What Changed: ### **New Approach:** - ✅ **Standalone form** instead of trying to inherit - ✅ Uses `res.config.settings` model (still works properly) - ✅ Styled to match Odoo settings UI - ✅ No xpath - no parsing errors - ✅ Won't conflict with other settings ### **Key Features:** ```xml
``` ### **Why This Works Better:** | Aspect | Inherited View (Old) | Standalone View (New) | |--------|---------------------|----------------------| | **Xpath errors** | ❌ Constant issues | ✅ None | | **Compatibility** | ❌ Version-dependent | ✅ Version-independent | | **Settings functionality** | ✅ Works | ✅ Works (same) | | **Visual consistency** | ✅ Matches Odoo | ✅ Matches Odoo | | **Maintenance** | ❌ Fragile | ✅ Stable | ### **Result:** - Opens a dedicated "Daily Report Configuration" settings page - Looks and works like Odoo settings - No interference with main Settings menu - The `execute()` method still saves properly to `ir.config_parameter` **This should upgrade without errors!** The tradeoff is it's a separate settings page instead of integrated into the main Settings menu, but it's much more reliable.

file.content:
<odoo>
    <record id="view_hr_department_notes_form_inherit" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">hr.department.form.inherit.notes</field>
        <field name="model">hr.department</field>
        <!-- Inherit the view that adds activities_table_tab; the base hr form has no such page. -->
        <field name="inherit_id" ref="employees_department_custom_fields.view_department_form_inherit_custom"/>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">
            
            <xpath expr="//notebook[@name='hr_department_form_tabs']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="closable">0</attribute>
            </xpath>
            <xpath expr="//notebook[@name='hr_department_form_tabs']" position="inside">
                    <page string="Library">
                        <field name="library_item_ids"
                                mode="list,form"
                                nolabel="1">
                            <list>
                            <field name="sequence" widget="handle"/>
                            <field name="name"/>
                            <field name="sharepoint_path"/>
                            <field name="doc_type"/>
                            <field name="tag_ids" widget="many2many_tags"/>
                            <field name="owner_id"/>
                            <field name="status"/>
                            <button name="action_open_sharepoint"
                                type="object"
                                string="Open"
                                icon="fa-external-link"
                                class="btn-link"/>
                            </list>
                        </field>
                    </page>

                    <page string="Notes" name="department_notes_tab">
                        <field name="department_notes_ids" context="{'default_department_id': id}">
                            <list editable="bottom">
                                <field name="note_taken_date"/>
                                <field name="note_taken_by" widget="many2one_avatar_user"/>
                                <field name="notes" widget="html" options="{'collaborative': True}"/>
                            </list>

                            <form string="Department Note">
                                <sheet>
                                    <group>
                                        <group>
                                            <field name="note_taken_date"/>
                                            <field name="note_taken_by" widget="many2one_avatar_user"/>
                                        </group>
                                    </group>
                                    <label for="notes" string="Note Content"/>
                                    <field name="notes" widget="html" nolabel="1" options="{'style-inline': true}"/>
                                </sheet>
                            </form>
                        </field>
                    </page>
            </xpath>

        </field>
    </record>
    <record id="view_department_form_inherit_email" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">hr.department.form.inherit.email</field>
        <field name="model">hr.department</field>
        <field name="inherit_id" ref="hr.view_department_form"/>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">
            <xpath expr="//field[@name='parent_id']" position="after">
                <field name="dept_email" widget="email"/>
            </xpath>
        </field>
    </record>
</odoo>

aiTitle: Add interview meetings tab to applicant form

content.text:
Done! I've updated the Activities view to match the notes pattern:

## Changes Made:

### **1. Added Row Numbering** 
```xml
<list ... numbering="1">
```
- Both "Planned" and "Done" lists now show row numbers (1, 2, 3...)

### **2. Changed to Descending Order (Newest First)**
```xml
<!-- Planned activities -->
default_order="date_deadline desc, id desc"  <!-- Changed from asc to desc -->

<!-- Done activities -->
default_order="completed_date desc, id desc"  <!-- Already desc -->
```

### **3. Added Separator Line at Top**
```xml
<separator string="Activities" class="mb-3"/>
```
- Adds a visual line/header at the top of the Activities tab

## Result:

**Planned Activities:**
- ✅ Row numbers: 1, 2, 3...
- ✅ Ordered by date descending (newest deadline first)
- ✅ Visual separator at top

**Done Activities:**
- ✅ Row numbers: 1, 2, 3...
- ✅ Ordered by completion date descending (most recent first)
- ✅ Already had correct order

Now the Activities tab matches the notes pattern with numbered rows, descending order (newest first), and a clear separator line at the top!

content.text:
I've updated the CSS to create row numbers as a separate "virtual" column instead of trying to add them inside the existing columns. This should work better:

## Changes Made:

### **New Approach:**
- Creates a **separate number column** using `tr::before`
- Adds a **"#" header** for the numbers column
- Numbers appear **before all other columns**
- Styled with gray background to stand out

### **What You'll See:**

```
#  | Open | Activity Type | Summary | ...
---+------+---------------+---------+----
1  | Open | To-Do         | ...     | ...
2  | Open | To-Do         | ...     | ...
3  | Open | To-Do         | ...     | ...
```

The row numbers now appear in their own dedicated column at the very left of the table, before the "Open" button column.

**Refresh the page or reload the module** and the row numbers should now be visible!

<odoo>
    <!-- sequence: metadata / future use; Odoo 18 form UI orders tabs by DOM position -->
    <record id="view_crm_lead_form_tab_ordering" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">crm.lead.form.tab.ordering</field>
        <field name="model">crm.lead</field>
        <field name="inherit_id" ref="crm.crm_lead_view_form"/>
        <field name="priority">99</field>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='activities_table_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">10</attribute>
            </xpath>
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='general_conditions_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">20</attribute>
            </xpath>
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='lead_notes_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">30</attribute>
            </xpath>
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='crm_opportunity_report']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">35</attribute>

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='appointments_page']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">40</attribute>
            </xpath>
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='certifications']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">50</attribute>
            </xpath>
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='checklist_tab']" position="attributes">

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='disclaimers_tab']" position="attributes">

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='bd_bid_intake']" position="attributes">

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='subcontractors_tab']" position="attributes">

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='bonding']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">100</attribute>

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='bd_follow_ups']" position="attributes">

            </xpath>
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='lead']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">1000</attribute>
            </xpath>
        </field>
    </record>

    <!-- Physical DOM order (what the web client renders) -->
    <record id="view_crm_lead_form_tab_ordering_moves" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">crm.lead.form.tab.ordering.moves</field>

            </xpath>
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='certifications']" position="after">
                <page name="checklist_tab" position="move"/>
            </xpath>
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='checklist_tab']" position="after">
                <page name="disclaimers_tab" position="move"/>
            </xpath>
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='disclaimers_tab']" position="after">
                <page name="bd_bid_intake" position="move"/>
            </xpath>
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='bd_bid_intake']" position="after">
                <page name="subcontractors_tab" position="move"/>

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='bd_follow_ups']" position="after">
                <page name="lead" position="move"/>
            </xpath>
        </field>
    </record>
</odoo>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<odoo>
    <record id="view_project_form_tab_ordering" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">project.project.form.tab.ordering</field>
        <field name="model">project.project</field>
        <field name="inherit_id" ref="project.edit_project"/>
        <field name="priority">99</field>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">

            <!-- HIDE SETTINGS TAB -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='settings']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="invisible">1</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- SET TAB SEQUENCE FOR ORDERING -->
            <!-- Only tabs from this module - others set in their own modules -->

            <!-- TEAM -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='TEAM']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">200</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- PROJECT CONTACTS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='contacts']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">400</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- CERTIFICATIONS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='certifications']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">700</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- DELIVERABLES -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='deliverables']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">800</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- MEETINGS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='project_meetings']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">1100</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- SUBCONTRACTORS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='subcontractors_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">1200</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- APPOINTMENTS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='appointments_page']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">2400</attribute>
            </xpath>

        </field>
    </record>
</odoo>

input.content:
# Project Tab Ordering

## Overview

This module provides deterministic, consistent ordering for all tabs on the Project form view. Without this module, tab order depends on module installation order, which can vary between environments.

## Current Tab Order

The tabs appear in this order (left to right):

1. **Deliverables** - Project deliverables and milestones
2. **Notes** - Project notes and comments
3. **Activities** - Planned and done activities
4. **Closing Checklist** - Project closing checklist items
5. **Team** - Project team members
6. **Contract Info** - Contract information and details
7. **Budget & SOW** - Budget and Scope of Work
8. **Project Contacts** - Project-related contacts
9. **Marketing** - Past performance and marketing info
10. **Quality Assurance** - QA records and documentation
11. **CAPA** - Corrective and Preventive Actions
12. **Board of Directors** - BOD-related information
13. **Bonding** - Project bonding information
14. **Incidents** - Incident reports
15. **Lessons Learned** - Lessons learned documentation
16. **Meetings** - Project meetings
17. **Subcontractors** - Subcontractor information
18. **Certifications** - Project certifications
19. **Appointments** - Project appointments
20. **Warranty** - Warranty information
21. **Settings** - Project settings (at the end)

## How It Works

The module uses two approaches to ensure tabs appear in the correct order:

1. **Sequence Attributes**: Sets `sequence` attributes on each tab (metadata for potential future use)
2. **Physical DOM Moves**: Physically moves tabs in the DOM tree to ensure correct rendering order

## Customizing Tab Order

To change the tab order:

1. Edit `views/project_tab_ordering.xml`
2. Update sequence numbers in the first `<record>` (keep gaps of 10 for future insertions)
3. Update the `position="move"` statements in the second `<record>` to match your desired physical order
4. Upgrade the module

## Dependencies

This module depends on all modules that add tabs to the Project form:

- `project` (base)
- `project_custom_fields`
- `project_notes`
- `project_kickoff_checklist`
- `project_past_performance`
- `quality_assurance`
- `capa`
- `bd_bod`
- `project_bonding`
- `incident_reports`
- `lessons_learned`
- `warranty`

**Note**: If new modules are installed that add tabs to the Project form, they should be added to the `depends` list and their tabs should be positioned in this module.

## Technical Details

- **Priority**: 99 for sequence setting, 100 for moves (ensures this runs after module-specific views)
- **Model**: `project.project`
- **Inherits**: `project.edit_project`

## Installation

1. Place the module in your addons directory
2. Update the app list
3. Install "Project Tab Ordering"
4. Refresh the browser

## Avoiding Circular Dependencies

If a new module needs to be added that creates a circular dependency:

1. Remove it from the `depends` list
2. Set that module to depend on `project_tab_ordering` instead
3. Add its tab positioning in this module

## License

LGPL-3.0 or later

Perfect! I've created the `project_tab_ordering` module similar to `crm_tab_ordering`. Here's what was created:

## 📁 Module Structure

```
project_tab_ordering/
├── __init__.py
├── __manifest__.py
├── README.md
└── views/
    └── project_tab_ordering.xml
```

## 🎯 Tab Order (Based on Screenshot)

The module orders tabs as follows:

| # | Tab Name | Module Source |
|---|----------|---------------|
| 1 | **Deliverables** | project_custom_fields |
| 2 | **Notes** | project_notes |
| 3 | **Activities** | project_custom_fields |
| 4 | **Closing Checklist** | project_kickoff_checklist |
| 5 | **Team** | project (base) |
| 6 | **Contract Info** | project_kickoff_checklist |
| 7 | **Budget & SOW** | project_kickoff_checklist |
| 8 | **Project Contacts** | project_custom_fields |
| 9 | **Marketing** | project_past_performance |
| 10 | **Quality Assurance** | quality_assurance |
| 11 | **CAPA** | capa |
| 12 | **Board of Directors** | bd_bod |
| 13+ | *Other tabs...* | various |

## 🔗 Dependencies (No Circular References)

```python
"depends": [
    "project",                    # Base
    "project_custom_fields",      # Core customizations
    "project_notes",              # Notes tab
    "project_kickoff_checklist",  # Checklist, Contract, Budget
    "project_past_performance",   # Marketing tab
    "quality_assurance",          # QA tab
    "capa",                       # CAPA tab
    "bd_bod",                     # Board of Directors
    "project_bonding",            # Bonding
    "incident_reports",           # Incidents
    "lessons_learned",            # Lessons learned
    "warranty",                   # Warranty
]
```

## ⚙️ How It Works

Like `crm_tab_ordering`, it uses **two approaches**:

1. **Sequence Attributes** (lines 10-88): Sets metadata sequence numbers
2. **Physical DOM Moves** (lines 90-180): Actually moves tabs in the DOM

## 🚀 Installation

1. **Install the module**: Update app list → Install "Project Tab Ordering"
2. **Refresh browser**: Hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) to see changes
3. **Check tabs**: Open any project → tabs should be in the specified order

## 📝 Customizing Order

To change tab order:

1. Edit `views/project_tab_ordering.xml`
2. Update **both** sections:
   - Sequence numbers (first `<record>`)
   - Move statements (second `<record>`)
3. Upgrade the module

## ✅ Benefits

- ✅ **Consistent** tab order across all environments
- ✅ **Predictable** UI for all users
- ✅ **Centralized** tab management
- ✅ **No circular dependencies** 
- ✅ **Easy to customize** - all in one file
- ✅ **Well documented** - README included

The module is ready to install and will establish the exact tab order shown in your screenshot!

aiTitle: Add interview meetings tab to applicant form

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: - Add owner field to recruitment checklist items to track who is responsible for each checklist activity - Add job_description_portal field to job positions for candidate portal display, separate from internal description - Improve interview invitation email content and structure with clear requirements section - Add validation before sending interview invites to ensure portal credentials exist - Fix project_dailys settings view that was replacing the entire Odoo settings menu - Consolidate CSS rules by moving HTML field min-height from project-specific module to global_style module, changing value to 15px - Add row numbering, descending order, and top separator line to project activities tab - Create project_tab_ordering module similar to crm_tab_ordering for consistent tab ordering - Verify all dependencies and create dependency map to avoid circular references 2. Key Technical Concepts: - Odoo 18 model inheritance and related fields - Many2one relationships for tracking ownership/responsibility - HTML fields for rich text content in portals - Transient models (wizards) for user decision workflows - View inheritance with xpath expressions for extending views - CSS counters with ::before pseudo-elements for row numbering - Physical DOM ordering vs sequence attributes in Odoo views - Module dependency management and circular dependency avoidance - Portal template structure and candidate-facing interfaces - Email template building with HTML formatting - res.config.settings pattern for module configuration 3. Files and Code Sections: **X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\hr_recruitment_enhanced\models\hr_applicant_checklist_item.py** - Added owner_id field to track responsibility ```python owner_id = fields.Many2one( 'res.users', string='Owner', help='Person responsible for this checklist item', tracking=True ) ``` **X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\hr_recruitment_enhanced\models\hr_job_checklist_template.py** - Added owner_id field to templates so ownership is copied to items ```python owner_id = fields.Many2one( 'res.users', string='Owner', help='Default person responsible for this checklist item', tracking=True ) ``` **X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\hr_recruitment_enhanced\models\hr_applicant.py** - Modified action_populate_checklist_items to copy owner from template ```python self.env['hr.applicant.checklist.item'].create({ 'applicant_id': self.id, 'template_id': template.id, 'name': template.name, 'sequence': template.sequence, 'upload_by': template.upload_by, 'owner_id': template.owner_id.id if template.owner_id else False, }) ``` **X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\hr_recruitment_enhanced\models\hr_job.py** - Modified action_populate_default_checklist to include owner when copying templates ```python self.env['hr.job.checklist.template'].create({ 'job_id': self.id, 'name': tmpl.name, 'sequence': tmpl.sequence, 'upload_by': tmpl.upload_by, 'owner_id': tmpl.owner_id.id if tmpl.owner_id else False, 'status': tmpl.status, 'sharepoint_url': tmpl.sharepoint_url, 'description': tmpl.description, }) ``` **X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\hr_recruitment_enhanced\views\hr_applicant_checklist_item_views.xml** - Added owner_id to list view (optional="show"), form view, search view, and group by options **X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\hr_recruitment_enhanced\views\hr_job_checklist_template_views.xml** - Added owner_id to template list/form views and search filters **X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\hr_recruitment_enhanced\views\hr_applicant_views_inherit.xml** - Added owner_id column to checklist items list within applicant form **X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\hr_recruitment_enhanced\models\hr_job.py** - Added job_description_portal field ```python job_description_portal = fields.Html( string="Job Description (Portal)", help="Job description visible to candidates in the portal" ) ``` **X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\hr_recruitment_enhanced\models\hr_applicant.py** - Added related field for easy access ```python job_description_portal = fields.Html( string='Job Description (Portal)', related='job_id.job_description_portal', readonly=True, help='Job description visible to candidates in the portal' ) ``` **X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\hr_recruitment_enhanced\views\hr_job_views.xml** - Added field to Job Posting tab ```xml ``` **X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\hr_recruitment_enhanced\views\hr_applicant_views_inherit.xml** - Added Job Description tab in applicant form ```xml ``` **X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\hr_recruitment_enhanced\views\portal_templates.xml** - Restructured to show job description at bottom after all forms and documents - Removed old job description from middle section - Added at bottom with better styling: ```xml

Job Description

``` **X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons\recruitment_interview_manager\models\interview_invite.py** - Improved _get_email_templates method with better structure: ```python # Interview Requirements section with clear heading candidate_body_html += """

Interview Requirements

Please complete the following items before your interview and upload all required materials to your Candidate Portal. Your login credentials will be included at the end of this email.

""" # Bulleted list format for each requirement test_instructions = "
    " if self.include_typing_test: test_instructions += """
  • Typing Test: Complete the typing test at https://www.typingtest.com/ and upload a screenshot of your completed results to the Candidate Portal.
    • """ # ... similar for excel, assessment, application # Better contact section with emojis candidate_body_html += """

    Questions or Need Help?
    If you have any questions about the interview process or need help confirming your attendance, please contact us:
